skill.name: Azure AI Content Understanding SDK for Python
skill.description: Multimodal AI service that extracts semantic content from documents, video, audio, and image files for RAG and automated workflows.
skill.summary: Extract structured content from documents, images, audio, and video using Azure AI Content Understanding SDK with prebuilt and custom analyzers.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation for the Azure AI Content Understanding SDK, an official Microsoft Azure service. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security issues detected (risk score: 0/100). The SKILL.md file provides usage examples for document, image, audio, and video analysis using legitimate Azure SDK methods. No executable code, network calls, or dangerous patterns present.

content.user_title: Extract content from documents, images, audio, and video with Azure AI
content.value_statement: Transform unstructured media files into structured, searchable content for RAG applications and automated workflows. Use prebuilt analyzers or create custom field extraction models with the Azure AI Content Understanding SDK.
actual_capabilities: Extract markdown content from PDF and image documents using prebuilt-documentSearch analyzer
Transcribe audio files with timestamped phrases using prebuilt-audioSearch analyzer
Analyze video content for transcripts, key frames, and summaries using prebuilt-videoSearch analyzer
Create custom analyzers with field schemas for domain-specific data extraction
Support both synchronous and asynchronous client patterns for different throughput needs
Authenticate using Azure DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity support
limitations: Requires Azure Content Understanding resource endpoint and valid credentials
Analysis operations are long-running and may take minutes for video or large files
File inputs must be accessible via URL or require separate upload handling
Custom analyzers must be created before use and count against resource quotas

use_cases.title: RAG Document Indexing
use_cases.description: Convert PDF documents, research papers, and technical manuals into markdown format for retrieval-augmented generation systems.
use_cases.target_user: AI engineers building knowledge base chatbots

use_cases.title: Meeting Intelligence
use_cases.description: Transcribe recorded meetings and webinars with speaker timestamps for searchable meeting notes and action item extraction.

use_cases.title: Invoice Processing Automation
use_cases.description: Extract vendor names, invoice totals, and line items from supplier invoices using custom field extraction models.
use_cases.target_user: Finance teams streamlining accounts payable workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Document Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this PDF document using the prebuilt-documentSearch analyzer and return the extracted markdown content. Document URL: {url}

prompt_templates.title: Audio Transcription with Timestamps
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe this audio file and provide all phrases with their start and end timestamps. Audio URL: {url}. Format output as: [start - end]: text

prompt_templates.title: Video Content Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this video to extract key frames, transcript phrases, and generate a summary. Video URL: {url}. Return: 1) Key frame descriptions with timestamps, 2) Full transcript, 3) Executive summary

prompt_templates.title: Custom Field Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom analyzer with fields: {field_schema}. Then analyze this document: {url} and extract values for each defined field. Return results as structured JSON with field names and extracted values.

output_examples.input: Analyze a research paper PDF using prebuilt-documentSearch
output_examples.output: Document analysis complete. Extracted 15 pages of markdown content including 3 figures, 2 tables, and 47 paragraphs. Content structured with proper headings and citations preserved.

Audio transcription complete. 1,247 words across 89 timestamped phrases. Key topics detected: project timeline, budget review, resource allocation. Full transcript available with speaker segmentation.

output_examples.input: Extract invoice fields from vendor PDF
output_examples.output: Custom analyzer extracted: vendor_name='Acme Corporation', invoice_total=15420.50, invoice_date='2026-01-15', line_items=[{description: 'Software License', amount: 12000}, {description: 'Support', amount: 3420.50}]

best_practices: Use begin_analyze with AnalyzeInput for all analysis operations and await poller.result() for completion
Access extracted content via result.contents[0] and check the content.kind property for type-specific handling
Prefer async client with azure.identity.aio for high-throughput scenarios requiring concurrent analysis jobs
anti_patterns: Do not call analyze() directly - always use begin_analyze() which returns a poller for long-running operations
Avoid accessing result.fields without first verifying the analyzer was configured with a field_schema
Do not use sync client for batch processing workflows that require analyzing multiple files concurrently

faq.question: What file formats does Content Understanding support?
faq.answer: Documents: PDF, images (JPEG, PNG), Office docs. Audio: MP3, WAV, M4A. Video: MP4, MOV. The prebuilt analyzers are optimized for specific content types.

faq.question: How long do analysis operations take?
faq.answer: Documents typically complete in seconds. Audio and video analysis are long-running operations that may take several minutes depending on media length and complexity.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with local files?
faq.answer: The examples show URL-based inputs. For local files, you must upload to accessible storage (Azure Blob Storage recommended) and provide the URL to the analyzer.

faq.question: What is the difference between sync and async clients?
faq.answer: Sync client uses blocking calls suitable for scripts and low-throughput scenarios. Async client (aio) enables concurrent operations and is recommended for high-throughput batch processing.

faq.question: How do I create a custom analyzer?
faq.answer: Use client.create_analyzer() with an analyzer_id, description, base_analyzer_id, and field_schema defining the fields to extract. The custom analyzer persists for repeated use.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: DefaultAzureCredential supports managed identity, service principal, CLI credentials, and environment-based authentication. Configure CONTENTUNDERSTANDING_ENDPOINT environment variable with your resource URL.
